source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: Median progression-free survival (mPFS) was 5.3 months in arm A versus 4.3 months in arm B.
finding.effect_summary: No significant difference in mPFS between arms A and B.

source_quote: Median progression-free survival (mPFS) was 5.3 months [95% confidence interval (CI) 4.3-6 months] in arm A versus 4.3 months (95% CI 3.5-5.5 months) in arm B [hazard ratio (HR) 0.78, 95% CI 0.55-1.10, P = 0.158].

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: Median overall survival (mOS) was 14.8 months in arm A versus 12.9 months in arm B.
finding.effect_summary: No significant difference in mOS between arms A and B.

source_quote: Median OS (mOS) was 14.8 months (95% CI 12.1-18.3 months) in arm A versus 12.9 months (95% CI 11 months-not evaluable) in arm B (HR 1.00, 95% CI 0.65-1.52, P = 0.983).

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: Patients with 'negative hyperselection' had significantly improved mPFS and mOS compared with 'positive hyperselection'.
finding.effect_summary: Significant improvement in mPFS and mOS for 'negative hyperselection' patients.

source_quote: These patients had significantly improved mPFS [5.35 months (95% CI 4.4-5.9 months) versus 3.65 months (95% CI 2.8-4.8 months); HR 0.62, 95% CI 0.42-0.92, P = 0.017] and mOS [15.0 months (95% CI 12.6-19.9 months) versus 11.1 months (95% CI 8.6-15.6 months); HR 0.61, 95% CI 0.39-0.97, P = 0.037] compared with patients having at least one pathogenic variant ('positive hyperselection').
